headline: Thursday and Friday are the week's standout summit days — and with an Extreme Heat Watch on, the high peaks are the cool play, not the valleys.
narrative: A hot, hazy stretch under an Extreme Heat Watch keeps low trailheads in the 80s while the summits stay 20–30°F cooler. Tuesday gives a solid window on the high Presidentials, Wednesday fogs them in completely (Washington reads 100% cloud), then Thursday–Friday clear out broadly across the range for the best hiking of the week before Saturday slides back downhill.

best_bet.why: Broad bald summit scoring 96 with only ~47% cloud and comfortable mid-70s — the week's best mix of big views, low fog, and modest hazard.

plan.label: Best all-around days
plan.recommendation: Thursday or Friday — Moosilauke, Carrigain, and the Franconia Ridge (Lafayette–Lincoln) all run 93–96 with low cloud. This is the week's clear standout; plan the big objective here.

plan.label: If you want the high Presidentials
plan.recommendation: Tuesday — Adams, Jefferson, and Washington clear into the 97–100 range, but Washington carries ~51 mph gusts and afternoon thunder risk. Go early and watch the sky.

plan.label: If you're out Wednesday
plan.recommendation: Skip the Presidentials — they're a guaranteed whiteout (Washington 100% cloud). Drop to a lower view peak: Chocorua, South Moat, or Black Cap (90–92), all below the deck.

plan.label: Beat the heat
plan.recommendation: With the heat watch, the summits are the refuge, not the lowland trails. Save the low ledges (Welch–Dickey, Chocorua) for early morning or a swim-hike; the valleys bake into the 80s by midday.

judgments: Washington's 97 on Tuesday undersells the danger: 51 mph gusts, ~53% afternoon thunder odds, and full above-treeline exposure. The score says 'go'; the conditions say 'early and cautious, with a turnaround plan.'
Wednesday's lower-peak scores (Chocorua 92, the Moats/Black Cap ~90) are the genuine pick that day — the high summits sit fully in the cloud regardless of what their raw number suggests.
The low ledges' high scores ride on heat: Welch tops out near 84°F midweek. Great for a short morning or a dip, rough for a sustained climb under the heat watch.
hazards: Extreme Heat Watch in effect — low trailheads and valleys are dangerously hot; carry extra water, start early, and remember the summits run 20–30°F cooler.
Presidentials midweek: gusts to ~51 mph and roughly 50% afternoon thunderstorm odds with full exposure above treeline — turn back if storms build.
Wednesday: the high peaks are fully fogged in (whiteout navigation risk on the open Presidential ridges).
model: Claude Opus 4.8 (this agent, on its daily run)
